description: Fix the instanceUrl configSchema field that is advertised to users for self-hosted PostHog but never consumed by posthog-api.ts — users who configure a self-hosted URL see no effect and tools silently target the wrong instance.
qualityChecks: cd plugins/posthog && npm run build && npm run type-check && npm run lint && npm run format:check

userStories.title: Use instanceUrl config in posthog-api.ts for API base URL
userStories.description: In plugins/posthog/src/index.ts, the plugin declares instanceUrl in configSchema with description 'The URL of your self-hosted PostHog instance'. However, posthog-api.ts never calls getConfig('instanceUrl') — all API calls use relative paths that are resolved against the current page origin. This means self-hosted users who configure their instanceUrl see no change. Add a base URL helper that reads getConfig('instanceUrl') and prepend it to absolute API calls so self-hosted instances work correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: posthog-api.ts calls getConfig('instanceUrl') to determine the API base URL
When instanceUrl is configured, API calls use that URL as the base (e.g., https://posthog.example.com/api/...)
When instanceUrl is not configured (empty/undefined), API calls fall back to relative paths (current behavior for cloud instances)
All tool API calls go through the base URL helper
userStories.notes:
Files:
- plugins/posthog/src/posthog-api.ts (add getApiBase() helper and use it in all API calls)

Context:
The posthog-api.ts file uses relative paths like `/api/environments/${teamId}/...`. These resolve correctly on cloud PostHog (app.posthog.com or eu.posthog.com). For self-hosted instances, the same relative paths resolve against the self-hosted domain, which should already work — but only if the browser is on that domain.

The urlPatterns in index.ts hardcode `*://us.posthog.com/*` and `*://eu.posthog.com/*`. When a user configures instanceUrl (e.g., https://posthog.example.com), the platform derives a new URL pattern `*://posthog.example.com/*` from the configSchema url-type field and injects the adapter into tabs on that domain. So the relative paths SHOULD work if the user is on their self-hosted domain.

This means the actual issue is more subtle: the adapter IS injected into self-hosted tabs (via derived urlPatterns), and relative paths DO work. But the API base is implicitly the current page origin, not the configured instanceUrl. If the user navigates to both us.posthog.com AND their self-hosted instance, the adapter runs on both, but each uses relative URLs (correct behavior per tab).

Before implementing, verify: does getConfig('instanceUrl') need to be used at all, or is the current relative-URL approach correct once the adapter is injected into the self-hosted tab?

If the relative approach is indeed correct (adapter injected by the platform into the right tab, relative paths resolve correctly), then the real fix is to clarify the configSchema description so users understand instanceUrl is for URL pattern matching, not for changing API targets. Update the description to: 'The URL of your self-hosted PostHog instance. Used to inject the adapter into your instance — leave empty to use PostHog Cloud.'

If on the other hand getConfig('instanceUrl') should be used to construct absolute API URLs (e.g., for cross-origin calls), implement the API base URL helper.

Read posthog-api.ts fully, then make the decision based on how the API URLs are constructed.
